app.name: CareerPath
app.tagline: Your Path to a Career in Japan

login.terms_prefix: By continuing, you agree to our

home.daily_goal_desc: Complete {count} lessons to keep your streak
home.ai_mock_interview_desc: Practice with an AI interviewer — Coming Soon!
home.partner_school_title: Study in Japan with Our Partner School
home.partner_school_desc: Learn on-site with full visa and career support.

mochimono.title: What to Bring — 持ち物チェックリスト
mochimono.subtitle: Prepare everything you need for the interview
mochimono.tip_text: The first impression is determined by preparation. Pack everything the night before. Even skills and motivation can't overcome being unprepared!

dansei.title: Men's Grooming — 男性の身だしなみ
dansei.subtitle: Master men's grooming for interviews
dansei.section2_title: Suit & Shirt — スーツ・シャツ
dansei.tip_text: Cleanliness is the most important factor. Iron your suit, polish your shoes, and check yourself in a full-length mirror before leaving.

josei.title: Women's Grooming — 女性の身だしなみ
josei.subtitle: Master women's grooming for interviews
josei.section1_title: Hair & Makeup — 髪型・メイク
josei.culture_note_text: Bring a spare pair of stockings! They tear easily, so always have an extra pair in your bag. Clear nail polish is OK, but keep nails short and neat. Remove all jewelry including earrings.

hyojo.title: Expression & Posture — 表情と姿勢
hyojo.subtitle: First impressions through expression and posture
hyojo.smile_desc: Smile naturally — slight teeth showing gives a bright impression.
hyojo.eye_desc: Look at the interviewer's eyes. Don't look down or away.
hyojo.react_desc: React expressively! Nod and show you're listening carefully.
hyojo.voice_tip2: Don't speak too fast — stay calm and speak slowly
hyojo.voice_tip3: Greet while looking at them, speak clearly, THEN bow

rirekisho.title: Resume Basics — 履歴書の基本
rirekisho.subtitle: Learn how to write a proper resume
rirekisho.rule_pen: Use black ink only. Never use pencil or colored pens.
rirekisho.rule_correction: No corrections allowed. Rewrite from scratch if you make a mistake.
rirekisho.rule_photo: 3×4cm, suit photo, within last 3 months. Plain background.
rirekisho.rule_date: Use the date you submit the resume, not the date you wrote it.
rirekisho.rule_era: Use Japanese era (令和) or Western year consistently throughout.
rirekisho.tip_text: Mistakes on your resume leave a very bad impression. Always have someone review it before submitting. Use formal Japanese throughout.

profile_qual.title: Profile & Qualifications — プロフィールと資格
profile_qual.subtitle: Fill in education, work history, and qualifications correctly
profile_qual.step1_text: Start with 中学校卒業 (junior high graduation). Write school name, department, and graduation year.
profile_qual.step2_text: List in chronological order. Include アルバイト (part-time jobs) if relevant. Use 入社/退社 format.
profile_qual.step3_text: List ALL qualifications with the date received. JLPT level, driving license, IT certs, etc.
profile_qual.step4_text: End each section with 以上 on a new line to indicate the list is complete.
profile_qual.tip_text: Fill in as many as possible. Include licenses from your home country too!

shibodoki.title: Motivation & Self-PR — 志望動機・自己PR
shibodoki.subtitle: Learn to promote yourself effectively
shibodoki.step1_en: Pick one strength from your self-analysis (e.g., teamwork, patience, language skills)
shibodoki.step2_en: Give a real story: "At my part-time job, I solved X by doing Y"
shibodoki.step3_en: End by explaining how you can contribute to THIS specific company
shibodoki.culture_note_text: In Japan, 志望動機 (motivation for applying) is one of the most important parts. Companies want to know: Why THIS company? Research the company before writing. Generic answers like "I want to work in Japan" are not enough — be specific about what attracted you.
shibodoki.tip_text: Make "Why this company?" crystal clear. Mention specific products, services, or values of the company that appeal to you.

sonota.title: Other Fields — その他の記入欄
sonota.subtitle: Fill in the remaining fields correctly
sonota.commute_meaning: Commute time — calculate door-to-door in minutes
sonota.dependents_meaning: Dependents — family members you financially support
sonota.culture_note_text: This section is where you write special requests. But be very careful — writing too many requests gives a bad impression.
sonota.ng_reason: Never write 特になし. Use 「貴社の規定に従います」 or 「貴社の規定に準じます」 instead.

nyutaishitsu.title: Entering & Leaving — 入退室のマナー
nyutaishitsu.subtitle: Learn the flow from entering to leaving the room
nyutaishitsu.enter_step1_desc: Knock on the door 3 times slowly. Wait for 「どうぞ」 before entering.
nyutaishitsu.enter_step2_desc: Open the door, step in, turn to face the interviewer, and greet with a bow.
nyutaishitsu.enter_step3_desc: Walk to the left side of the chair. Stand straight and introduce yourself.
nyutaishitsu.enter_step4_desc: Do NOT sit until told 「お掛けください」. Then say:
nyutaishitsu.enter_step5_desc: Sit shallow on the chair. Back straight, hands on knees. Bag on the floor beside the chair.
nyutaishitsu.leave_step1_desc: Stand up from the chair and move to its side.
nyutaishitsu.leave_step2_desc: Bow at 45 degrees beside the chair (最敬礼).
nyutaishitsu.leave_step3_desc: Walk to the door, turn around, and bow again.
nyutaishitsu.leave_step4_desc: Open the door, step out, close it gently. Stay polite until you leave the building.

aisatsu.title: Greetings & Self-Intro — 挨拶と自己紹介
aisatsu.subtitle: Master self-introduction for interviews
aisatsu.scenario_a2: Thank you very much. Excuse me. (Sit down)
aisatsu.template_en: Nice to meet you, my name is ___. I'm from ___. I've been in Japan for ___ years. I study ___ at ___ school. I enjoy communication and can contribute to teamwork. Thank you.

henji.title: How to Respond — 返事の仕方
henji.subtitle: Learn proper responses in interviews
henji.voice_desc: Speak in a loud, clear voice. Don't mumble.
henji.smile_desc: Always smile! A smile solves everything in Japanese interviews.
henji.nod_desc: Nod while listening. Show you're engaged with the conversation.
henji.scenario_a1: Yes! My name is ___. Nice to meet you.
henji.scenario_a2: Yes, no problem! I'll do my best!
henji.scenario_a3: Yes, may I ask one? Is there anything I should study before joining?
henji.scenario_q4: Thank you for your time. That's all.

online.title: Online Interview Setup — オンライン面接の準備
online.subtitle: Prepare your camera and microphone
online.section1_title: Preparation Checklist — 準備チェック
online.tip_text: Be ready 5 minutes before. Test camera, mic, and internet. Close unnecessary apps. Dress in full interview attire — even for online interviews!

shitsumon1.title: Common Questions ① — よく聞かれる質問(前)
shitsumon1.subtitle: Prepare for frequently asked interview questions
shitsumon1.q1_en: What is your dream for the future?
shitsumon1.a1_tip: Be specific and connect it to working in Japan. Show you have a long-term vision.
shitsumon1.q2_en: How many years do you want to stay in Japan?
shitsumon1.a2_tip: Companies want long-term employees. Show commitment: "I want to build my career here."
shitsumon1.q3_en: What did you study at school?
shitsumon1.a3_tip: Mention your major and connect it to the job. If unrelated, explain what skills you gained.
shitsumon1.q4_en: What are your strengths and weaknesses?
shitsumon1.a4_tip: For weaknesses, always explain what you're doing to improve. Turn negatives into positives.
shitsumon1.q5_en: Why did you choose to work in Japan?
shitsumon1.a5_tip: Mention specific aspects of Japanese culture, technology, or industry that attracted you.
shitsumon1.q6_en: Please tell us why you applied to our company.
shitsumon1.a6_tip: Research the company! Mention specific projects, products, or values. Never give a generic answer.
shitsumon1.tip_text: Always include specific episodes. "I like Japan" is not enough — explain a specific moment that made you want to work here.

shitsumon2.title: Common Questions ② — よく聞かれる質問(後)
shitsumon2.subtitle: Prepare for the remaining frequent questions
shitsumon2.q7_hint: What are your hobbies? Connect hobbies to skills: teamwork, focus, creativity.
shitsumon2.q8_hint: What part-time work do you do? Highlight customer service, responsibility, teamwork.
shitsumon2.q9_hint: Difficulties in Japan? Show how you overcame challenges. Resilience matters.
shitsumon2.q10_hint: Happy moments in Japan? Show appreciation for Japanese culture and people.
shitsumon2.q11_hint: What do you value at work? Mention teamwork, quality, communication, learning.
shitsumon2.q12_hint: Any questions for us? ALWAYS prepare 2-3 questions! Ask about training, team culture, etc.
shitsumon2.ng_reason: NEVER say this! Always prepare 2-3 questions. It shows genuine interest. Ask about work content, team culture, or training programs.

naitei.title: Post-Offer Steps — 内定後の手続き
naitei.subtitle: What to do after receiving a job offer
naitei.step1_desc: Receive and verify the offer letter. Check salary, position, start date, and conditions carefully.
naitei.step2_desc: Tell the company you accept. Do this quickly — within the deadline. Call or email formally.
naitei.step3_desc: Switch from student/designated activities visa to work visa (就労ビザ). The company will help with paperwork.
naitei.step4_desc: Prepare: 学歴証明書, 卒業証明書, 成績証明書, 資格合格証, パスポート, 在留カード
naitei.step5_desc: Start your new career! Continue improving Japanese and learn industry-specific terms during the waiting period.
naitei.tip_text: Start visa paperwork immediately. Processing takes 1-3 months. Your school and company will help, but YOU are responsible for gathering documents from your home country.

kokorogamae.title: Mental Preparation — 心構え
kokorogamae.subtitle: Face your interview with confidence
kokorogamae.yaruki_desc: Motivation and fighting spirit — companies want people who TRY.
kokorogamae.genki_desc: Greet energetically! This alone makes a huge difference.
kokorogamae.junbi_desc: Your mindset is also preparation. Be confident!
kokorogamae.example_en: The difference between passing and failing is decided by just a small margin.

practice.title: Practice
practice.coming_soon: Coming soon! Practice your interview skills here.
practice.score_good: 👍 Good job! {score}/{total} — Review the ones you missed.
practice.score_retry: 📖 {score}/{total} — Review the lesson and try again!
